summary: Iteration 2 (BMF engine). Backend: 16/16 pytest passing including BMF save v1, evolution to v2 with history (state_changes populated, replaced decisions moved to history.decisions), Deploy Engine returning <context> with relevance object (matched_prompt True with prompt, False empty), Deploy 400 on memory_version 0, workspace/{id}/memory full BMF shape, workspace/{id}/export returning .bmf filename + base64 package + bmf_version 1.0, plus regression (folders, search, optimize, recent, deployments, auth). Frontend: seeded session via localStorage.bounce_token; New Memory save flow completes to 'Memory Ready' with structured result; Deploy modal renders <context> and subtitle 'memory v1' as required.

ui_bugs.issue: Emergent 'Made with Emergent' badge in bottom-right intercepts pointer events over the primary CTA when the Save panel is open at 1920x1080 — Playwright native click times out; JS .click() works. Same issue was documented in iteration_1; still unresolved.

issues: Duplicate technology entries can appear (e.g., 'Supabase' listed twice) because deploy.build_context concatenates frameworks+libraries+dependencies+integrations without a final dedup. Cosmetic.

action_items: (Optional) Dedupe tech list in deploy.build_context before ranking to avoid 'Supabase, Supabase' in Technologies.

(Optional) Raise Emergent badge z-index issue or add pointer-events:none to the badge overlay so save-memory-btn is natively clickable — cosmetic only, JS click works fine but real users see the CTA covered by the badge on 1080p screens.
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.jsx is 504 lines and packs Recent/Folders/Search/Deployments/Connect/Settings/SavePanel/DeployModal — approaching the 700-line threshold; split into /app/frontend/src/views/*.jsx.
bounce/bmf.py evolve() mutates the current BMF in place — safer to deep-copy at entry so a failed save doesn't leave a partial state in memory (though the DB write only happens after evolve returns).
deploy.build_context: sel_tech concatenates 4 arch lists without a final _dedup_extend; duplicates leak into the <context>.

OPENROUTER_API_KEY is a real credit-limited key committed in /app/backend/.env — must be rotated before public push. Extract call is capped at max_tokens 3200 so partial extractions are expected; evolution is deterministic Python and still produces memory_version increments (validated).
test_credentials: Backend pytest self-seeds via uuid suffix (see fixture 'seeded'). UI test used mongosh-seeded session: TOKEN=test_session_ui_72aa5bcf USER=user_smoke_cdc9c8dd.
seed_data_creation: Pytest fixture seeds 1 user + 5 folders + 1 session per worker; teardown deletes all TEST_ + user data. UI run seeded 1 user + 5 folders + 1 session via mongosh (see auth_testing.md).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest layout: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. The pytest.ini forces `-n 2 --dist loadscope`, so tests inside a single class share a worker. Shared setup between classes MUST go through fixtures (e.g., session-scoped evo_folder) not pytest.module attributes, since attributes don't cross workers. seeded fixture uses uuid.hex[:12] suffix (NOT time.time()*1000) to avoid cross-worker session_token collisions. BMF evolution assertions accept any of: replaced decision in history, active decision changed, or state_changes populated — because LLM extraction can be partial (max_tokens 3200) but the evolve() logic is deterministic Python. Frontend save-memory-btn requires JS .click() due to Emergent badge overlay at bottom-right.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues in this iteration. Two known cosmetics carried over: (a) Emergent badge intercepts save-memory-btn (workaround: JS click); (b) deploy.build_context concatenates arch lists without dedup — duplicates leak into rendered <context> Technologies section.
